wc_history_home: Switzerland have appeared at 13 World Cups, reaching the quarter-finals in 1934, 1938, and 1954, and have been a consistent presence in the round of 16 in the modern era (2014, 2018) with a last-16 exit to Portugal in 2022.
wc_history_away: Algeria have appeared at five World Cups (1982, 1986, 2010, 2014, 2026), with their best run being the Round of 16 in 2014, and their most famous moment being the stunning 2-1 upset of West Germany in 1982.
group_context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
strengths_home: Experienced, defensively disciplined squad with quality throughout
High-press system generates turnovers and transition goals
Granit Xhaka's leadership and control of midfield tempo
strengths_away: Prolific attacking threat led by Mahrez and Amoura
Strong qualifying form — eight wins in CAF qualifying, best-ever campaign
Dangerous on the counter-attack with technically gifted wide players
team_style_home: Switzerland under Murat Yakin play a disciplined 4-2-3-1 built around Granit Xhaka as the deep-lying playmaker, using a high man-to-man press to win the ball high up the pitch and launching quick transitions on the flanks through pace of Dan Ndoye and Ruben Vargas.
team_style_away: Algeria under Petkovic deploy a 4-3-3 or 4-4-2 built on combination play, wide exploitation, and counter-attacking flair, with Mahrez and Amoura providing creative and goal-scoring threat.
recent_news: Algeria intensified preparations ahead of its crucial FIFA World Cup Round of 32 clash against Switzerland after securing third place in Group J with a thrilling 3-3 draw against Austria. Switzerland bounced back with a 4-1 win over Bosnia and Herzegovina and a 2-1 win vs. Canada to win Group B.
key_players_away: Mohamed Amoura (striker, top scorer in qualifying with 10 goals)
context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
